arts.ttl: Martin Odegaard points blame for Arsenal's brutal defeat to West Ham
arts.sum: Arsenal endured a tricky weekend after being defeated by West Ham having had a man sent off but captain Martin Odegaard is under no illusions where the fault for the reverse lies

arts.ttl: MOTD analysis: Potter's 'outstanding' West Ham dent Arsenal title hopes
arts.sum: Ex-England captain Alan Shearer digests West Ham's outstanding performance against Premier League title challengers Arsenal.

arts.ttl: “All clicked” | West Ham crush Gunners title hopes with superb win
arts.sum: West Ham crush Arsenal’s title hopes for a third year in a row with an immense 1-0 win. Although they’re far from our main rivals, there’s perhaps no team that is more satisfying to beat than Arsenal. Their fans meltdown, Arteta pulls out every excuse that he can, and it always results in some quality... The post “All clicked” | West Ham crush Gunners title hopes with superb win appeared first on The West Ham Way.

arts.ttl: Arsenal's Myles Lewis-Skelly breaks unwanted Premier League record as Mikel Arteta reacts to West Ham red card
arts.sum: Arsenal's Myles Lewis-Skelly sets an unwanted Premier League record with his red card in Saturday's disheartening 1-0 defeat to West Ham United at the Emirates.

arts.ttl: Arteta says Arsenal’s performance was below the mark needed to win the title
arts.sum: 'I never felt we were at the level required,’ says manager West Ham’s Graham Potter: ‘A really positive day for us’ A “very, very angry” Mikel Arteta conceded that Arsenal’s performance as they slumped to defeat against West Ham had not been up to the standard required to win the Premier League and said his players must “feel the pain” before their trip to Nottingham Forest in midweek. Jarrod Bowen’s 50th Premier League goal sealed three points for Graham Potter’s side as the substitute Myles Lewis-Skelly was sent off in the second half. It leaves Arsenal still eight points behind Liverpool, with Arne Slot’s side having an opportunity to extend their lead when they face Manchester City on Sunday. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Arsenal, Mikel Arteta defended amid striker crisis as January transfer failures laid bare
arts.sum: Arsenal legend Ray Parlour explains to Sports Mole why the Gunners not signing a striker in the January transfer window could still work out for them.

arts.ttl: Paul Merson says the Premier League title is 'one million per cent' heading to Liverpool after Arsenal's loss to West Ham as he claims Mikel Merino played like a 'fish up a tree' in his new position
arts.sum: Liverpool will travel to Manchester City on Sunday but Merson claimed that even a 7-0 defeat for the leaders would not be enough to reignite Arsenal's hopes of winning the title.

arts.ttl: 'I'm very, very angry' - Arteta after Arsenal hopes suffer 'big blow'
arts.sum: The Premier League title race may not be done yet - but Mikel Arteta was "very, very angry" after Arsenal's loss to West Ham.
